articles.title: Man kills seven of his children, and an eighth child, in Louisiana mass shooting
articles.summary: The children, aged from one to 14 years old, were killed in a shooting that police are describing as a "domestic disturbance".

articles.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says Iranian ship seized
articles.summary: Energy markets have seen wild swings since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February.

articles.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
articles.summary: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

articles.title: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
articles.summary: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.

articles.title: New Zealand declares state of emergency in Wellington as floods hit
articles.summary: Footage online shows vehicles submerged, trees uprooted and houses hit by landslides.

articles.title: Rumen Radev's party wins Bulgarian election
articles.summary: With 87% of the vote now counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in parliament.

articles.title: At least 25 killed in firecracker factory blast in India
articles.summary: Mostly female workers have been killed in the blast, which occurred in Tamil Nadu state.

articles.title: Thousands of Parisians evacuated as WW2 bomb detonated
articles.summary: Residents within a 450m radius of the device were told to leave their homes while it was neutralised.

articles.title: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say

articles.title: Ontario Premier Doug Ford agrees to sell $21m private jet following outcry
articles.summary: After confirming the purchase on Friday, Ford drew critcism from political opponents who called it a "gravy plane" and "out of touch".
